Module 4:
Creating Prelaunch Content,
Part 2
!
Executive Summary
When creating prelaunch content videos, you can do screencap or full
motion video. Screen capture is often easier than full motion for many
reasons. There are lots of logistics and technical pieces with full motion.
For one, it can be a lot easier to deliver a script with a screen cap video. If
this is your first launch or you're just starting out you may want to script
your video tightly. If you're going to deliver from a script with a screen
capture you could read it off your computer screen.
With screencap, you can record the audio first, and then you can do the on-
screen video recording after. A lot of people dislike being on camera and
have many worries. Just practice getting in front of the camera, you’ll
eventually get better.
If that’s the case and you can’t get around it, screencap is the way to go.
It’s generally easier for the viewer to connect with you if it’s full motion
video.
It’s harder to connect with someone if it's a screen capture video.If you had
never seen me in full motion, you just wouldn't have as close a connection
with me as you do.
!

-- 1 of 2 --

If you need to edit something into a full motion video, you can actually
record it as a screencap, then put a little section in the middle of your full
motion video.
Screen capture can be a lot faster to create, but if you want to use fancy
animations it does get slower and it's a lot more effort to create.
Important: you can mix full motion with screen capture video - I’ve done this
a lot for my launches.
!
